qa_pairs.question: In JupyterLab, what is the recommended method for properly ending an RStudio session to ensure that all allocated cluster resources (CPU, memory, GPU) are released?
qa_pairs.answer: To properly end an RStudio session and release cluster resources in JupyterLab, you must use the menu item `File > Log Out` on the JupyterLab browser tab. Simply quitting RStudio or closing the RStudio and JupyterHub tabs in your browser will not release these resources or end the underlying Slurm job.

qa_pairs.question: What is MLflow, and how does it assist users working with machine learning projects in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: MLflow is an open-source platform purpose-built to assist machine learning practitioners and teams in handling the complexities of the machine learning process. It focuses on the full lifecycle of machine learning projects, ensuring each phase is manageable, traceable, and reproducible, allowing users to track their AI experiments effectively.

qa_pairs.question: What functionalities does the native terminal session in JupyterLab provide for users?
qa_pairs.answer: The native terminal session in JupyterLab allows users to run Bash commands, submit jobs, and edit files directly on the remote compute node without requiring an SSH connection. It provides access to remote filesystems like `/home`, `/project`, and `/scratch`, and enables running compute tasks. The terminal also supports standard copy-and-paste operations for text.
